label: DigitalOcean
description: Manage DigitalOcean Droplets, Spaces, App Platform, databases, and other cloud resources.

form.title: Connect DigitalOcean
form.subtitle: Authorize Anton to manage your DigitalOcean cloud resources.

methods.label: Personal Access Token
methods.description: Paste a DigitalOcean personal access token. Quickest setup — generate a token in the API panel and paste it here. No OAuth app required.
methods.how_to:
## Generate a Personal Access Token

1. Log in to [cloud.digitalocean.com](https://cloud.digitalocean.com).
2. Click **API** in the left sidebar.
3. Under the **Tokens** tab, click **Generate New Token**.
4. Give it a name (e.g. `Anton`) and set an expiry if desired.
5. Choose the **Scopes**: select `read` and `write` for the resources Anton needs to manage (Droplets, Spaces, App Platform, etc.).
6. Click **Generate Token**. Copy the token immediately — it is shown only once.
7. Paste it into the **Personal Access Token** field below.

## Notes

- The token is shown only once. If you lose it, revoke it and generate a new one.
- Prefer scoped tokens (read-only if Anton only needs to query resources) to limit exposure.
- Tokens can be revoked at any time from the same **API → Tokens** panel.

fields.name: access_token
fields.label: Personal Access Token
fields.description: From cloud.digitalocean.com → API → Tokens → Generate New Token. Shown only once — copy it before closing the dialog.

methods.label: OAuth 2.0 (Bring Your Own App)
methods.description: Use a DigitalOcean OAuth application to authorize Anton. Requires creating an OAuth app in the DigitalOcean control panel and supplying its client credentials.
methods.how_to:
## Set up a DigitalOcean OAuth Application

1. Log in to [cloud.digitalocean.com](https://cloud.digitalocean.com).
2. Click your avatar (top right) → **API** → **OAuth Applications** tab.
3. Click **Register Application**.
4. Fill in a name (e.g. `Anton`) and set the **Callback URL** to `http://127.0.0.1` (Anton uses a local redirect).
5. Click **Save**. Copy the **Client ID** and **Client Secret** shown on the next screen.
6. Paste both into the fields below and click **Submit**.
7. Anton opens your browser to DigitalOcean's consent screen — approve the requested scopes.

## Notes

- The client secret is shown once. Store it securely.
- Revoking the OAuth application in the control panel will immediately invalidate Anton's access.
- For simpler setup, use the **Personal Access Token** method instead.

fields.name: client_id
fields.label: OAuth Client ID
fields.description: From cloud.digitalocean.com → API → OAuth Applications → your app's Client ID.

fields.name: client_secret
fields.label: OAuth Client Secret
fields.description: Paired with the Client ID. Shown once when the app is created — store it safely.
